title: Sanjay Raut claims Congress MLC offered ₹20 cr to switch loyalties; legislator refutes allegation
content: Sanjay Raut did not elaborate further, nor did he specify who offered Dhiraj Lingade money and which party the Congress MLC was supposed to join

title: Experts emphasise E20 compliant, thoroughly tested amid raging concerns
content: It said that, as per its service protocol, the fuel tank and fuel lines were drained, thoroughly cleaned, and the vehicle was refilled with Standard E20 fuel

title: Day 16 of CJP protest: Sonam Wangchuk loses six kg as fast enters eighth day
content: Samyukta Kisan Morcha (SKM), which had earlier extended support to the CJP agitation, is now scheduled to send a delegation to the Jantar Mantar on July 7

title: V. Sivankutty slams Kerala CM’s ‘reverse remittance’ remarks on migrant workers
content: Objection on Kerala Chief Minister’s recent observation that large amounts of money earned in Kerala are flowing to other States through reverse remittances and that migrant workers contribute mainly to State economy through taxes generated from liquor consumption

title: Kerala CPI(M) leader M.V. Jayarajan says expelled leaders can return to party fold if they ‘rectify their mistake’
content: Senior leader appears to hold out an olive branch to CPI(M) dissidents-turned-UDF Independent MLAs T.K. Govindan and V. Kunhikrishnan, representing the Payyannur and Taliparamba Assembly constituencies respectively

title: BJP masking its failures with ‘so-called Hindutva’, says Lalu
content: Lalu Prasad alleged that the BJP is attempting to push the country backward through authoritarian means by capturing constitutional institutions and sidestepping the Constitution

title: V. Sivankutty urges Kerala government to resolve administrative paralysis at Thiruvananthapuram Corporation
content: Sivankutty alleges that attempts by NDA-led Corporation’s leadership to protect Vazhottukonam ward councillor R. Sugathan, who is under judicial remand after being arrested under provisions of Kerala Anti-Social Activities Prevention Act, has disrupted day-to-day functioning of the local body

title: U.S. actions as ‘world police’ can cause diplomatic strife, U.S. DoJ on why it is dropping Adani case
content: In May 2026, the DoJ first sought approval from the Eastern District Court of New York to drop the cases entirely

title: Residents of Narasingapuram municipality demand bus shelters
content: A passenger said that on both sides of the Salem-Chennai road that passed via the municipality, people had to wait on roads for the buses to go towards Chennai or Salem

title: Upalokayukta pulls up hospital, civic body over lapses in Kodagu
content: While inspecting the general wards and the women and children’s wards in Kodagu Institute of Medical Sciences, Justice Phaneendra stressed the need for better sanitation and instructed doctors to adhere to duty schedules to prevent public complaints.

title: Scuffle between ticket examiner and lawyer causes stir among commuters at Egmore railway station
content: Ticket Examiner Salma Begum asked the lawyer if she possessed a valid ticket, to which the lawyer replied that she had come to see off a friend. Then Salma Begum asked to see a platform ticket, but the lawyer did not have one

title: Haryana Human Rights Commission takes cognisance of custodial torture, extortion allegations against police
content: The Commission further directed that if the allegations are found to be true, responsibility shall be fixed on the concerned police officials, and an Action Taken Report, along with the findings of the inquiry, shall be submitted at least one week prior to the next date of hearing.

title: Scholars told to pursue research on societal, industrial needs
content: The conference provided a platform for scholars to present their research findings, receive expert feedback, and foster interdisciplinary collaborations

title: Puducherry plans AI Centre of Excellence
content: The initiative, backed by the India AI Mission, aims to enhance research capabilities, foster innovation, and integrate AI solutions across various sectors in Puducherry

title: Transport Department to consult Tiruppur with private bus operators on July 7 to address complaints of ‘unilateral’ fare revision
content: The bus operators plying services between Erode and Coimbatore and Tiruppur and Coimbatore were found by the authorities to have increased the fares “unilaterally” by ₹5 to ₹10 a ticket, based on the distance

title: Minister B. Rajkumar inspects ongoing projects in Cuddalore
content: Mr. Rajkumar also inspected the construction of a sewage treatment plant at Devanampattinam at an estimated cost of ₹43.45 crore and an Animal Birth Control (ABC) centre at Pachaiyankuppam.

title: CITU condemns layoffs at U.S.-based medical coding firm; calls for stronger labour protections
content: CITU will pursue all available legal remedies, including raising the matter as an industrial dispute before the Labour Commissioner and the Labour Courts, says former Labour Minister V. Sivankutty

title: Madras HC calls for order closing corruption case against former Minister C. Vijayabaskar, educationist Ishari K. Ganesh
content: The issue relates to a 2022 suo motu DVAC FIR, regarding corruption in issuing ‘essentiality certificate’ to Vels Medical College, having been closed in 2024

title: Government issues stern notice to Meta on child sexual abuse material in Instagram ads: sources
content: “MeitY has ordered Instagram to disable all ads and content promoting and facilitating access to CSEAM,” the sources said, adding that the Ministry also demanded a detailed explanation within seven days

title: Inside India’s language conundrum
content: The CBSE’s move to implement the National Education Policy 2020’s three-language formula from Class 6 has caused confusion across the country. The transition has led to teacher losses, curriculum disruptions, and concerns from parents, students, and foreign embassies. Maitri Porecha reports on the many challenges in implementing the three-language formula in a country as diverse as India

title: After Operation Sindoor, trust in ‘made-in-India’ defence platforms got enhanced: Rajnath Singh
content: Rajnath Singh reiterated that "today, our defence production has reached over ₹1.78 lakh crore, and about 8-9 years ago, it stood at approximately ₹46,000 crore"

title: I&B Ministry steps up pressure on Telegram, seeks proactive action against piracy
content: Telegram has been accused in multiple court cases of delayed or little action in response to reports of piracy

title: West Bengal to recover triple damages for property vandalism: CM Adhikari
content: If an accused fails to pay the damages, the recovery will be made even by auctioning their properties, says Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari

title: Government withdraws emergency gas curbs imposed during Hormuz disruption as LNG supplies normalise
content: The Ministry said the situation has since improved, with a ceasefire in place, negotiations underway and maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz resuming.

title: U.P. BJP tries to shore up women voter numbers with SIR leaving wide gender gap
content: In many districts, women who have married into districts other than their own still find that they have not been added to either their native district or that of their in-laws, said a source from the BJP

title: All allies united, NDA to focus on repeating 2017 poll win: BJP leaders after meeting Nitin Nabin
content: Leaders of the BJP allies in the State said the meeting would focus on strengthening the coalition, expanding its organisational base and evolving a strategy to ensure the NDA's return to power in the State

title: BJP will register a decisive victory in U.P., says party chief Nitin Nabin
content: Mr. Nabin, who reached Lucknow on a two-day visit, added that he was overwhelmed by the respect received in the land of Lord Shri Ram.

title: Stale food, cockroaches, rat droppings found at Hyderabad restaurants during food safety inspections
content: Officials noted an inadequate utensil washing area where hot water sanitisation was not being followed, along with flaking walls and ceilings in the vegetable cutting section

title: Prepare contingency plan for sowing amid delayed monsoon: Official
content: Officials told to ensure adequate stocks of seeds and fertilizers and prepare for drinking water scarcity if dry spell continues.

title: Bring Ram Janmabhoomi Trust within RTI purview: CPI(M) leader John Brittas
content: CPI(M) Rajya Sabha leader says trust was constituted through government action and should be subject to transparency provisions under the RTI Act

title: Pandavani legend Teejan Bai dies at 70
content: Teejan Bai was widely regarded as the foremost exponent of Pandavani, the traditional folk art form of Chhattisgarh that narrates episodes from the Indian epic Mahabharata

title: Kurnool district police intensify enforcement to curb road accidents
content: Police have registered 21,977 various traffic violations in the last six months during the enforcement drives

title: Jaladhara works increase groundwater levels in Kurnool district
content: The Collector said out of 3,714 works sanctioned under NREGS, 3,707 were initiated, and 3,446 have been completed

title: Removal motion against Justice Varma in legal grey area after his resignation
content: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla confirmed the report on corruption charges against Justice Yashwant Varma will be presented in the monsoon session, despite his resignation. The House will decide the next steps after the report's tabling, which could happen on the session's first day. Justice Varma faces allegations of financial irregularities following a fire incident at his Delhi residence last year.

title: 'Unhappy with marriage': Newlywed woman, lover kill husband, dump body in canal
content: A 21-year-old man's body found in a Rewari canal was allegedly murdered by his wife and her associates due to her extramarital affair. The wife, Tannu, and an accomplice, Hariom, have been arrested. The victim, Monu, was reportedly lured to a village and then thrown into the canal. Investigations are ongoing to apprehend the remaining accused and determine the exact cause of death.

title: 'SIT should take their help': VHP chief's dig at opposition amid Ram Mandir row
content: ​​The latest escalation comes after VHP International President Alok Kumar wrote to the Investigation Officer handling the alleged Ram Temple embezzlement case, urging verification of allegations made by several Opposition leaders, including Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, AAP national convener Arvind Kejriwal, AAP leader Sanjay Singh and Samajwadi Party leader Ram Gopal Yadav.

title: Dropping Samson for Sooryavanshi 'most bizarre selection': Manjrekar blasts India
content: Taking to X after the match, Manjrekar welcomed the 15-year-old's debut but wondered why Samson had been left out of the playing XI.

title: Delhi: Jobless man kills wife with tawa after argument, then confesses at police station
content: A 48-year-old man confessed to police in Southwest Delhi that he killed his wife with a tawa following a late-night argument. The accused, who claims to be undergoing schizophrenia treatment, surrendered at a police station Sunday morning. Investigators are verifying his medical claims and exploring the couple's financial distress and recent arguments as potential motives for the fatal incident.

title: First 25 mn US kids signing up for Trump Accounts to get personal donation from Dell couple
content: Dell Technologies founder and CEO Michael Dell and his wife Susan announced on July 4 that they'll give $250 each to the first 25 million qualifying American children who sign up for a Trump Account, the new government-backed investment vehicle that launched the same day. The pledge builds on the Dells' earlier $6.25 billion commitment. Here's how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for the Dell donation, and what the money can eventually be used for.

title: US tops the global economic chart—but for how long? Deutsche Bank flags biggest risk
content: America's mounting debt is now the primary threat to its global economic dominance, surpassing external competition.  Deutsche Bank warns that persistent deficits and soaring interest payments are eroding a key advantage.  While the US possesses enduring strengths, the nation faces imminent fiscal challenges, including the potential exhaustion of Social Security and Medicare funds, which will demand urgent attention from future administrations.

title: Successor in hiding? Three of Ali Khamenei's sons attend funeral, Mojtaba skips
content: During the state funeral prayers for his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and four family members, Iran’s new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, chose to remain out of the public eye. Citing security threats following an attack, his absence has sparked considerable speculation. As his brothers and key officials attended, eyes are turned towards Mojtaba's anticipated return amidst reports of personal injuries and ongoing security concerns.

title: 'I've watched him bat in India': Bethell's big verdict on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made history as India's youngest T20I debutant, impressing England's Jacob Bethell despite a modest 14 runs. Bethell, who starred with an unbeaten 76 to guide England to victory, lauded Sooryavanshi's raw talent.  The young Indian's performance, even in defeat, generated significant buzz, highlighting his promising future in cricket.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Zuckerberg admits Meta misjudged AI progress
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged internal restructuring hasn't yielded expected results, particularly in AI agent development, admitting executives 'miscalculated' timelines. Despite significant layoffs and AI investments, progress has been slower than anticipated. However, Zuckerberg anticipates substantial AI benefits within three to six months. Separately, the company addressed mouse-tracking software, assuring no employee data was used for AI training and confirming future opt-in policies.

title: 'Since Mbappe didn't pay attention, I lost temper': Gill on no-handshake drama
content: Orlando Gill admitted he "lost my temper" after Kylian Mbappe ignored his post-match handshake following France's 1-0 World Cup win over Paraguay national football team. Gill threw the ball at Mbappe after the final whistle, while the France captain defended his team's response to Paraguay's physical tactics after scoring the decisive penalty.

title: Katherine Heigl to sell 25-acre Utah mountain home as family starts new chapter
content: Actress Katherine Heigl and musician Josh Kelley are selling their sprawling 25-acre Utah mountain estate for $10.6 million after nearly two decades. The custom-built home, a hub for their family life and creative pursuits, is being left behind as they embrace a new chapter in a smaller, nearby Victorian farmhouse.

title: Watch: Two 2,000-pound bison clash on Yellowstone road as tourists watch
content: Yellowstone National Park witnessed a dramatic scene as two massive bull bison clashed on a road during their mating season, halting traffic. This powerful display of dominance, captured on video, highlights the raw strength of these 2,000-pound animals. Park officials urge visitors to maintain a safe distance, emphasizing that bison are wild and can become aggressive, especially during this time of year.

title: US-Canada ties: CEO of 'Canada's Palantir' sees support for military investments
content: Canada's defense tech startup, Dominion Dynamics, secured C$139 million in funding, aiming to bolster the nation's defense industry. CEO Eliot Pence believes public support for military investment is strong, driven by global tensions and a need for modern capabilities. The company focuses on Arctic technologies and advocates for faster procurement and a more experimental approach to defense innovation, emphasizing domestic production over mere purchasing.

title: Prashant Kishor takes on BJP bastion, to contest Bihar's Bankipur bypoll
content: Prashant Kishor, founder of the Jan Suraaj Party, will contest the July 30 Bankipur Assembly by-election in Bihar, marking his electoral debut. This move comes as a significant challenge to the BJP's long-held stronghold. Kishor views the bypoll as a referendum on the current state government, actively campaigning to amplify people-centric issues. The BJP remains confident of retaining the seat.

title: Dalal Street outlook: TCS, crude oil and more, what's driving stock market this week?
content: Indian markets brace for a pivotal week as the June-quarter earnings season kicks off with TCS on July 9. Investors will scrutinize results and management commentary on demand and AI. Global cues, including US Fed minutes and crude oil price stability, alongside domestic monsoon progress, will also shape market sentiment. Foreign investor activity is another key factor to watch.

title: PCB sacks Shan Masood, hands Test captaincy back to Babar Azam
content: Babar Azam has been reappointed as Pakistan's Test captain, leading the team against the West Indies and England in crucial ICC World Test Championship matches.  The squads feature four new faces, offering them a chance to debut.  Pakistan aims to climb the WTC standings from their current ninth position during these upcoming overseas assignments.

title: 'Saving Rs 30,000 a month': Corporate couple reveals how moving to Manali changed everything
content: A corporate couple's move to Manali for a slower pace of life unexpectedly led to significant monthly savings of over ₹30,000.  Without intending to cut costs, reduced rent, no commute, and fewer impulse buys like office dinners and coffee runs contributed to the surplus.  This unexpected financial bonus has sparked widespread discussion about the benefits of remote work and the hidden expenses of city living.

title: Ram Mandir funds row: Theft peaked during Maha Kumbh, warnings landed on deaf ears, reveals probe
content: An organized insider operation is suspected in the alleged theft of donations for the Ram temple, with an employee identified as a central figure. Investigators are examining evidence suggesting daily cash siphoning and suspicious behavior captured on CCTV. The probe also scrutinizes the handling of precious metal donations, with significant cash recoveries reported from several accused.

title: Renting a house? HRA eligibility, tax benefits and key rules explained
content: Salaried individuals renting homes can significantly reduce their taxable income by claiming House Rent Allowance (HRA) under the old tax regime. This exemption, available only to those paying rent and receiving HRA as part of their salary, is calculated based on actual allowance, rent paid minus 10% of salary, and a percentage of salary depending on the city.

title: Missile vs rocket: What really sets them apart?
content: Rockets and missiles, often confused, are distinct weapons. Rockets, with ancient origins, provide high-volume, affordable firepower for area saturation. Missiles, born from WWII's V-2, are precision-guided, capable of striking specific targets with advanced technology. While guided rockets blur lines, true missiles possess sophisticated guidance for diverse battlefield roles, making both indispensable for modern warfare's complementary strengths.

title: Bigger than the Pentagon? Egypt unveils 22,000-acre military command hub
content: On July 4, Egypt introduced 'The Octagon', a significant leap in military infrastructure aimed at consolidating defense capabilities. This expansive headquarters combines advanced command, logistics, and emergency management systems powered by state-of-the-art C4I/AI technology. The facility is expected to play a crucial role in enhancing strategic decision-making and addressing regional security concerns, drawing parallels to the iconic Pentagon.

title: India's top IT firms set for muted Q1 as AI spending fails to lift growth: Report
content: India's top IT firms anticipate a slow start to FY27, with growth likely to remain subdued as clients focus on cost savings over major tech investments. While AI adoption is rising, it's funded by efficiency gains, not budget increases. Margins are expected to hold steady due to currency benefits and cost control. Long-term prospects remain positive, driven by AI complexity and modernization needs.

title: Why England players have been allowed to use Viagra ahead of Mexico World Cup clash
content: England's World Cup preparations have taken an unusual turn as players are reportedly being offered Viagra to combat the effects of Mexico City's high altitude. The medication's ability to widen blood vessels could aid circulation in the thinner air, a factor explored by the medical team. This move comes as England faces a formidable Mexican side at the Estadio Azteca, a venue where they boast an impressive record.

title: 'Why should we not kill the man ... ?' Angry slogans against Trump at Khamenei's funeral
content: Chants of 'Death to America!' echoed at Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral as a performer called for Donald Trump's demise. Hundreds of thousands mourned the late Supreme Leader, displaying anti-US and anti-Israel sentiment. This comes amid ongoing negotiations with Washington, highlighting deep-seated animosity. The funeral, delayed due to recent conflict, also saw prayers led by Ayatollah Jafar Sobhani, with key Iranian officials in attendance.

title: Wife of retired IAF Wing Commander dies after jumping from multi-level car park in Noida
content: A Noida woman, identified as Situ Talwar, tragically fell from the Sector 18 multi-level car park on Saturday evening. Preliminary reports suggest she had been battling depression for two decades. Police were alerted via Dial 112 and she was taken to a private hospital where she was pronounced dead. Her family has been informed, and a post-mortem is underway.

title: Pakistan's public money a privilege? World Bank report reveals stark fiscal faultlines
content: Pakistan's fiscal decentralisation efforts have failed to bridge regional spending gaps, with provincial capitals like Quetta and Lahore receiving significantly more per person than their own provinces.  Despite budget surpluses in some areas, poorer regions face underinvestment.  Concerns also arise over education and healthcare spending not yielding desired results, alongside widespread financial irregularities uncovered by auditors.

title: Aamir Khan & Gauri Spratt officially married: The duo ties knot in Bandra
content: Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt married in an intimate Bandra ceremony, attended by Ira, Junaid Khan and friends. The couple, dating since 2024, moved in together before deciding to wed. This is Aamir's third marriage, following Reena Dutta and Kiran Rao, with whom he continues co-parenting and running Paani Foundation.

title: ‘Vote chori, seat chori, chanda chori’: Congress attacks BJP over Ayodhya donation row
content: The Congress has accused the BJP-RSS of a "scam" involving alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations, demanding a Supreme Court-monitored probe. Party leader Jairam Ramesh criticized the Prime Minister and Home Minister's silence, linking the issue to past funding practices. An FIR has been registered, with eight arrests made and cash recovered as the investigation continues into the alleged siphoning of "thousands of crores."

title: ‘People can be killed but ideals can't’: Iran condemns Trump's 'one shot' threat
content: Iran has fiercely condemned US President Donald Trump's remarks about potentially eliminating its leadership, accusing America of lacking "civilisation, history, or honour." Trump's comments, made in an interview, questioned the sincerity of mourners at the funeral of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and suggested a swift strike could eliminate Iran's top officials. The funeral itself has drawn massive crowds, projected by Iran as a display of national unity and defiance.

title: Cargo ship under attack 'by unknown assailants' in Red Sea, says British military
content: A cargo ship faced an attack in the Red Sea near Yemen's coast, the UK military confirmed Sunday. The vessel reported being targeted by unknown armed individuals. Investigations are underway, with no immediate details on the ship or any damage. While Houthi rebels have previously threatened such actions, no group has claimed responsibility yet. The incident raises concerns amid recent pirate activity in the Gulf of Aden.

title: 'Tea laced with sleeping pills': Hunt for 32 missing police guns uncovers murder plot
content: A shocking double life has been exposed in Faridabad, where a 22-year-old ITI trainee, Monu, arrested for stealing 32 guns from a police station, has confessed to strangling his cousin last July. He and his girlfriend, Sarita, allegedly drugged and then smothered Shivam before dumping his body in a canal. Divers are now searching the canal for the victim's remains.

title: Aamir Khan-Gauri Spratt wedding: First photo of couple as husband and wife out
content: Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt married in an intimate Pali Hill ceremony, with Spratt in ivory lace and Khan in a cream kurta. Khan had confirmed a simple registered marriage with close family and friends. This is his third wedding, following Reena Dutta and Kiran Rao, with whom he shares cordial co-parenting ties.

title: India's office market faces supply hurdle as leasing dips in April-June
content: India's office market saw a slight dip in leasing activity during the April-June quarter, primarily due to a scarcity of premium spaces. Despite global uncertainties, demand remains strong, with international firms continuing to invest in India, especially through global capability centers. Developers are now expected to shift focus back to commercial projects to meet future needs and support sector growth.

title: Shooting in US on fourth of July: 8, including 4 children, injured in Brooklyn
content: Tragedy struck Coney Island on Friday night as a shooting left at least eight people injured, including four young children. The incident occurred around 10:37 pm, with victims ranging from six to 37 years old. While most are in stable condition, a 21-year-old woman is critically injured. Police have recovered a firearm but no arrests have been made as investigations continue into the disturbing event.

title: New state, old script: Infighting returns to haunt Congress's Punjab poll plan
content: Punjab Congress faces a deep-rooted crisis fueled by persistent infighting and a delayed response from the high command. Despite Rahul Gandhi's call for unity, internal rifts are widening, with senior leaders expressing discontent over leadership decisions. This recurring pattern of internal conflict and inaction has historically led to electoral defeats, raising serious doubts about the party's prospects in the upcoming elections.

title: Axar Patel creates history, becomes first Indian spinner to...
content: Axar Patel has achieved a historic milestone, becoming the first Indian spinner to bag 100 T20I wickets. The all-rounder reached the landmark during the second T20I against England at Old Trafford, dismissing England captain Harry Brook. Since his 2015 debut, Patel has been a consistent performer for India in white-ball cricket, boasting an impressive record.

title: No radar needed: After GAGAN, India to get first satellite-guided helicopter approach
content: India celebrates a groundbreaking achievement in aviation with the successful launch of its first satellite-based Point-in-Space (PinS) approach procedure specifically for helicopters. Created by the Airports Authority of India and sanctioned by the Directorate General of Civil Aviation, this advancement facilitates precise navigation and landing through satellite signals, significantly improving safety and supporting operations in all weather conditions.

title: Escape routes sealed, more troops in J&K: Army's hunt for 2 LeT terrorists enters Day 2
content: Security forces are continuing a two-day anti-terror operation in Shopian's Meemandar area, intensifying their search for two suspected Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorists. The duo, identified as local residents Latif and Zakir, were spotted in a dense orchard and engaged security forces in a gunfight. Additional troops have been deployed to seal escape routes, with the operation ongoing despite challenging foliage.

title: Market recap: Top-10 firms add Rs 1 lakh crore in m-cap; Airtel, Bajaj Finance lead gains
content: Dalal Street concluded the past week on a high note, with top companies collectively adding over Rs 1 lakh crore to their market cap. Bharti Airtel and Bajaj Finance led the gains, buoyed by strong domestic economic data and positive global sentiment. While most of the top 10 firms saw their valuations climb, Larsen & Toubro and Reliance Industries experienced notable dips.

title: 'You make Mbappe seem like a dictator': France coach after win over Paraguay
content: Didier Deschamps defended Kylian Mbappe against criticism of his leadership following France's 1-0 World Cup win over Paraguay national football team. Deschamps insisted Mbappe's public image "doesn't correspond at all to reality" and praised France's discipline during a heated encounter, which was decided by Mbappe's second-half penalty.

title: Mbappe and France show they can win ugly in Paraguay slugfest
content: On Day 24, Mbappe and France put their hands through s**** while Morocco break Canadian hearts. Read this and more in today’s Offside.

title: As Mamata takes charge after Chandrima's resignation, BJP says 'TMC no longer exists'
content: West Bengal minister Dilip Ghosh claimed the Trinamool Congress is facing a "wave of resignations" and is effectively defunct, following Chandrima Bhattacharya's exit from all party posts. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has reassumed leadership amidst internal turmoil and alleged BJP intimidation. Ghosh questioned Bhattacharya's tenure and Banerjee's budget preparation methods, while Bhattacharya cited hurt over Banerjee's accusations regarding Trinamool Bhavan's control.

title: 'Spare a thought for Samson': Ex-cricketer reacts to India dropping World Cup hero
content: Ambati Rayudu defended Sanju Samson after his T20I exclusion, reminding fans of Samson's T20 World Cup Player of the Tournament award.  While welcoming 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut, Rayudu emphasized Samson's recent match-winning contributions. Samson's omission follows a single low score, sparking debate despite Sooryavanshi's promising, albeit brief, innings. England leads the series after a victory.

title: UK, Japan and Italy pump $6.1 billion into sixth-generation GCAP fighter
content: Edgewing, a collaborative venture formed by BAE Systems, Leonardo, and JAIEC, has secured a remarkable £4.6 billion contract to spearhead the development of a state-of-the-art sixth-generation fighter jet for the UK, Italy, and Japan. This investment ignites the next stage of the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), striving for operational capability by 2035.

title: Ram temple donation row: VHP asks SIT to verify claims made by Opposition leaders
content: VHP said that the statements of Opposition leaders will further help the investigative agencies to probe the allegations on Ram Temple donation theft

title: Gaganyaan mission: ISRO successfully conducts first SOLVE ground test
content: ISRO is developing a solid motor based SOLVE as a test platform to carry out Integrated Parachute Tests for validation of deceleration system of Gaganyaan’s Crew Module under various test conditions.

title: How does WhatsApp’s proposed username feature compare to Telegram’s? | Explained
content: Government says the feature could ‘facilitate impersonation and identity spoofing, including impersonation by permitting the adoption of usernames closely resembling those of genuine persons or institutions’

title: Pune building's compound wall collapses after rains; 14 vehicles damaged
content: "The residential society is located on Jambhulwadi Road in Katraj. The compound wall of the society collapsed onto the parking shed following incessant heavy rainfall. Several vehicles, including two-wheelers and four-wheelers, were damaged," a fire official said

title: Two Kashmir Shia clerics ‘disallowed’ to travel to Iran for Ali Khamenei’s final rites
content: J&K Anjuman-e-Sharie Shian president Aga Syed Hassan Al-Mosavi Al-Safavi was stopped by Bureau of Immigration; Shia cleric Masroor Abbas Ansari was also “denied a passport”

title: Tribal children in Maharashtra send postcards to CM seeking free education for kids up to 18
content: The children wrote postcards to convey their message to Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis as part of an awareness campaign organised by a tribal rights organisation, said officials of the organisation

title: Another batch of pilgrims on way to Amarnath shrine
content: The batch, including 1,310 women and 22 children, left the Jammu base camp in two separate convoys at 3 a.m. and 3.40 a.m., the officials said.

title: Telangana SIR: Political parties save the day as BLOs navigate unfamiliar territories to decide electors’ fate
content: In majority of the areas, the BLOs are clueless about the areas and houses to be covered; drawn from several departments, they are unable to understand the vague maps given to them on the mobile app

title: Six fishermen missing after boat capsizes off Visakhapatnam coast
content: "One fisherman has been rescued by a cruise ship, while search operations are underway for the remaining six," Vizag Fishing Boats Association president Lakshman Rao told PTI

title: ‘One Nation, One Election initiative would fracture country’s federal compact’
content: Conclave on ‘One-Nation One-Election, Federalism and Citizenship’ also raises concern on SIR, citizenship and ECI functioning

title: News in Frames: Fastest hooves first on the track
content: Widely regarded as one of Maharashtra’s most prestigious bullock cart races, the Hindkesari Bailgada Sharyat once again drew a massive turnout in Pedgaon

title: "Zero Tolerance": Meta To NDTV After Being Summoned Over Child Abuse Ads
content: The government demanded immediate corrective action against the "algorithmic amplification" of child sexual abuse material (CSAM)

title: 'Believe In Yourself': Meghalaya Chief Minister Conrad Sangma's Interaction With Student Wins Hearts
content: The exchange took place at Pine Mount School during the inauguration of the Meghalaya Teacher Training Academy, the She Thrives initiative and a newly developed boarding facility.

title: Gwadar Explained: Why Pak's Chinese-Backed Port Keeps Coming Under Attack
content: For the people of Gwadar, stability isn't an abstract goal. It's the chance to send a child to school without worrying about a sudden deadline of violence

title: China's New Ethnic Unity Law Raises Alarm Over Overseas Crackdown
content: The law legally consolidates President Xi Jinping's constant efforts to establish a national identity and promote the idea of a single "Chinese nation".

title: From Zia To 'Holy War', State Policy Built Pak's Extremist Ecosystem
content: Pakistan treats radicalisation as something that happened to Pakistan rather than something the state itself cultivated

title: "Needs To Be Strengthened": Maharashtra Food Regulator Chief On Manpower
content: In an exclusive interview with NDTV, Tukaram Munde said the foremost responsibility of the FDA is to ensure that every citizen gets safe food and safe medicines.

title: "Mentally Unstable": Government Sources Slam Pak's Khawaja Asif For Attack On PM Modi
content: The Seychelles government categorically rejected suggestions that the award was fabricated or created for PM Modi's visit.

title: Video: Karnataka Bus Without Functioning Headlights Driven Using Phone Torch
content: The video showed the driver operating the vehicle on a pitch-dark road without headlights while the conductor used a faint mobile phone torch in an attempt to help navigate

title: Watch: Trump Displays One Of Oldest US Flags At 'America250' Event
content: Unlike the well-known Betsy Ross flag, which has the stars arranged in a circle, this flag has the stars placed in three rows in a 4-5-4 pattern, Trump said.

title: Another Monsoon Death In Mumbai, Man Dies After Tree Falls On Him Amid Rain
content: It is the third such case in recent times. Days ago a schoolboy was killed after a tree broke off and crushed a school bus

title: Drone Delivery Takes Off In India, But Can It Replace The Last-Mile Rider?
content: Routine medical deliveries, warehouse transfers, emergency supplies and rural shipments are likely to move to the skies first.

title: Ali Khamenei's 3 Sons Attend Funeral In Tehran, Successor Mojtaba Skips
content: State television showed three of Ali Khamenei's sons, Mostafa, Meysam and Masoud Khamenei, standing behind their father's coffin during funeral prayers at Tehran's Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla

title: Trump Earns $636 Million As Memecoin Investors Lose $3.8 Billion: Report
content: The report found that 988,905 crypto wallets, representing roughly two out of every three buyers of the $TRUMP memecoin, were at a loss by the end of June.

title: Agra Woman Fed Husband 20 Sleeping Pills Before Killing, Burying His Body In Bathroom
content: For several days, whenever neighbours and relatives asked Ruby about Surendra's whereabouts, she would unhesitatingly say he was out and would return soon.

title: Trump's Patriotism vs Partisan Politics Goof-Up At US 250th Birthday Celebrations
content: Donald Trump leaned into partisan territory unusual for an Independence Day address, which presidents typically use as a moment to unify the country.

title: Condom Ad During Cricket Match Is "Adult Entertainment": Trinamool MP
content: "This is an adult advertisement. Children watch the match with their parents," Trinamool Congress MP and former cricketer Kirti Azad said

title: Red Alert In Mumbai Amid Heavy Rain, Civic Body Urges People To Stay Indoors
content: In view of the forecast, the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has appealed to people to remain alert and venture outdoors only if absolutely necessary.

title: Opinion: Anticipating A New Era In Nepal-India Relations
content: Geography, civilization, deep people-to-people ties, and a shared commitment to democratic ideals provide a stable foundation for the relationship between India and Nepal

title: On Camera, Punjab Man Visiting In-Laws To Reconcile With Wife Set On Fire By Brother-In-Law
content: Lovepreet Singh went to his in-laws' house to bring back his estranged wife but died after his brother-in-law allegedly set him on fire in Punjab's Tarn Taran

title: Tinted Glass, 'CATCHM3' Number Plate: 26 Lakhs Found Inside BMW In Lucknow

title: 'Stop the ads immediately': Centre's stern warning to Meta over child abuse content
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More
